title: Langshiang Falls
tagline: Langshiang Falls — heritage city of Meghalaya

overview.short: The Langshiang Falls is located near the village of Sangriang, 24 kilometres (15 mi) from Nongstoin, in West Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya.
overview.description: The Langshiang Falls is located near the village of Sangriang, 24 kilometres (15 mi) from Nongstoin, in West Khasi Hills district in the Indian state of Meghalaya. It can also be seen from Mawpon village. The total height of the falls is generally quoted at around 337 metres (1,106 ft). However, that needs to be confirmed.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Nongkhnum River Island
topPlaces.description: Nongkhnum River Island is the biggest river island in the Indian state of Meghalaya. The island is formed by the river Wah Kynshi on the west and south and river Namiliang on the north and east.

topPlaces.name: Nongstoin
topPlaces.description: Nongstoin (IPA: ˈnɒŋˌstɔɪn) is the headquarters of West Khasi Hills district in the state of Meghalaya in India. As of the 2011 census, it had a population of 28,742.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Nongstoin
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Nongstoin (Latin: Nongstoinen(sis)) is a diocese comprising of three districts of Meghalaya , namely West Khasi Hills , Eastern West Khasi Hills and South West Khasi Hills with the seat located in the town of Nongstoin at the Cathedral of St. Peter the Apostle .

items.note: Nongkhnum River Island is the biggest river island in the Indian state of Meghalaya. The island is formed by the river Wah Kynshi on the west and south and river Namiliang on the north and east.
Nongstoin (IPA: ˈnɒŋˌstɔɪn) is the headquarters of West Khasi Hills district in the state of Meghalaya in India. As of the 2011 census, it had a population of 28,742.

The Roman Catholic Diocese of Nongstoin (Latin: Nongstoinen(sis)) is a diocese comprising of three districts of Meghalaya , namely West Khasi Hills , Eastern West Khasi Hills and South West Khasi Hills with the seat located in the town of Nongstoin at the Cathedral of St. Peter the Apostle .

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Langshiang Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Langshiang Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Langshiang Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shillong Airport (Umroi) (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Guwahati Railway Station (97 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Langshiang Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Langshiang Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Langshiang Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Langshiang Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Langshiang Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nongkhnum River Island, Nongstoin, Diocese of Nongstoin.
